label: Table 4
title: Comparison of OCD patients with and without behavioural addictions on clinical characteristics of OCD.
source: Rai et al., 2022, Psychiatry Research Communications
footnote: © Mean ± standard deviation. BIS = Barratt Impulsivity Scale. β BIS subscales (attentional, motor, planning). Psychiatric comorbidities and patterns use n (%). Generalized Anxiety Disorder row: p reported as '< 1.000' in paper.

columns.role: Clinical or demographic feature being compared inside the OCD sample only

columns.header: OCD with BA N=105 n (%) or mean±SD
columns.role: 105 patients who crossed ≥1 behavioural addiction cut-off

columns.header: OCD without BA N=45 n (%) or mean±SD
columns.role: 45 patients who did not cross any cut-off on those screens
Chi-square for yes/no style rows; t-test for numeric rows like age or scale scores
Two-tailed p-value: is this difference larger than we would routinely expect by chance?

cellGuide: Everyone here has OCD; the split is “also screened positive on any behavioural questionnaire” versus “screened negative on all.” The table shows whether those two patient flavours differ on age, sex, OCD severity (Y-BOCS), impulsivity (BIS), or comorbidity counts. Hover headers for reminders.
anomalies: See content/ambiguities.json: BIS Attentional row statistic vs p may be inconsistent in the published table.
